info.title: VerdantForged TEE Broker API
info.summary: REST API for the VerdantForged TEE broker (eu-west-1).
info.description: Submit attested LLM jobs to a TEE-backed worker, poll status, fetch encrypted artifacts, and register new skills. The broker is a cheap always-on control plane (t3.small) that holds the queue, per-job LLM tokens, and Stripe payment lifecycle. The worker (m6a.xlarge, AMD SEV-SNP) is launched on demand and terminated after a configurable idle buffer. The real LLM API key never enters the worker — the worker receives per-job ephemeral tokens and calls the broker's /v1/llm/chat/completions proxy. **Live broker:** https://verdant.codepilots.co.uk

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Submit jobs, poll status, fetch artifacts

get.description: Returns 200 whenever the daemon is responding. Reports whether a worker is currently running (workers are launched on demand and terminated after an idle buffer, so `worker: false` is normal at idle). No auth.

Returns the broker's region, pricing model, supported skill catalog, and the worker's SEV-SNP attestation report (when populated). The attestation block carries the 1184-byte SEV-SNP report, the cert chain to the AMD root, the enclave's X25519 public key, the chip and family IDs, and the OpenShell policy hash. Callers SHOULD pin `attestation.min_measurement` to detect worker tampering and `attestation.policy_hash` to detect egress-policy widening. No auth.

post.description: Submits a text or encrypted file job. The one-time `job_access_token` authorizes every subsequent job operation and must be retained by the client; the internal worker LLM token is never exposed. File jobs enter `awaiting_worker`, bind an X25519 input key into verified SEV-SNP report_data, then expose encrypted S3 PUT URLs through authenticated status polling. Idempotent replays do not reissue the access token.

400.description: Invalid request body or Stripe PI

429.description: Per-IP rate limit or daily token cap exceeded

get.description: Returns the job record. During `awaiting_inputs`, also returns the attestation-bound worker input key and fresh presigned PUT URLs. On completion, result files are encrypted to `result_pubkey` and exposed through authenticated artifact routes.

401.description: Missing or invalid job access token

post.description: After uploading every ciphertext to its presigned PUT URL, verifies object sizes and the bound worker key, writes the canonical worker envelope, and transitions the job to queued.

401.description: Missing or invalid job access token

409.description: Inputs missing, wrong state, size mismatch, or worker key changed

get.description: Returns the output manifest with 15-minute presigned download URLs. Files use x25519-hkdf-sha256-chacha20poly1305-v1 and S3 objects expire after 24 hours.
Returns a 302 redirect to a presigned S3 GET URL. The filename must appear in the signed manifest.

302.description: Redirect to presigned S3 GET URL

404.description: Job, artifacts, or filename not in manifest

get.description: Returns every distinct skill name with its latest version. Built-in stubs (`code-review`, `summarize`, `photo-glow-up`) are advertised via `/v1/discover` and do NOT appear here unless explicitly registered. No auth.

post.description: Registers a new skill (or a new version of an existing one). Exactly one of `prompt_template` or `wasm_ref` must be provided. Resource limits are bounded above the built-in stubs (default 256 MB / 60 s / 10M fuel) and capped below 4 GB / 600 s / 100M fuel to prevent a compromised publisher from exhausting the broker. **Auth (required):** `Authorization: Bearer <BROKER_SKILLS_API_KEY>`. If the server's key is unset the endpoint returns 503.

Location.description: Canonical URL for the registered skill

409.description: Skill already registered at this version

503.description: Skill registration is disabled (BROKER_SKILLS_API_KEY not configured on server)

get.description: Returns a single skill. `ref` is either `name` (latest version) or `name@version`. No auth.

post.description: Workers call this endpoint with an internal per-job `llm_token` carried only in the worker envelope. The broker validates it, applies account caps, forwards a whitelist-stripped request to the configured upstream, and records usage. This token is never returned to requesters.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired LLM token

get.summary: Get token usage for a job
get.description: Returns cumulative LLM usage for a job. Requires the client job-access token.

LlmToken.description: Internal per-job worker token. It is placed only in the EFS worker envelope and is never returned to clients.

JobAccessToken.description: One-time client credential returned only by the initial successful POST /v1/jobs response. Stored server-side as a SHA-256 digest.

worker.description: True when an SEV-SNP worker is currently running.

min_measurement.description: SHA-256 of the worker instance ID (fallback) or the SEV-SNP measurement when available. Pin this to detect worker tampering.

policy_hash.description: SHA-256 of the OpenShell egress policy YAML. Pin this to detect policy widening (egress destinations allowed for the broker).

report_data.description: 128 hex characters. File workers bind the input-key digest into the first 64 characters.

cert_chain.description: Base64 DER cert chain to AMD root. Empty array when the report is empty.

enclave_pubkey.description: Worker X25519 public key (base64, 32 bytes). Used by clients to encrypt inputs to the enclave. Empty until the worker has emitted its attestation JSON.

worker_ed25519_pubkey.description: Worker Ed25519 public key (base64, 32 bytes). Verifies worker_signature and sandbox.image_digest_sig.

nemoclaw_version.description: NemoClaw version captured by the worker at sandbox onboard time.

nemoclaw_image.description: Docker image reference used by the NemoClaw sandbox.

nemoclaw_image_digest.description: Docker content digest for the NemoClaw image, typically sha256:<64 hex>.

attestation_source.description: `tsm_configfs` for file-capable attested workers; fallback workers cannot accept encrypted file jobs.

client_req_id.description: Caller-chosen idempotency key. Same value on a retry returns the original job.

encrypted_skill.description: Skill name (or registered skill identifier). For registered skills, the broker looks up `wasm_manifest_hash` from the catalog; for built-in stubs it falls back to `sha256(skill_name)`.

encrypted_data.description: Arbitrary opaque input payload (string). Worker treats as plaintext from the enclave perspective.

requester_sig.description: Hex/0x-prefixed Ed25519 signature over `sha256(skill) | sha256(input) | result_pubkey | stripe_pi_id | timestamp`. Pass `"0x"` for the demo path; production deployments can set `BROKER_REQUIRE_REQUESTER_SIG=1` to reject unsigned jobs.

requester_pubkey.description: Optional Ed25519 public key (hex/base64). When supplied alongside a non-`"0x"` `requester_sig`, the broker verifies the signature.

result_pubkey.description: Base64 32-byte X25519 public key. The worker encrypts the LLM output to this key. Use `"0x"` for demo (no encryption).

stripe_pi_id.description: Stripe PaymentIntent ID. Validated against Stripe (status must be `requires_capture` or `succeeded`). In demo mode (no STRIPE_SECRET_KEY), any `pi_*` string is accepted.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp used in the requester signature canonical payload.

webhook_url.description: Optional. When set, the broker POSTs the job result to this URL on finalize. Must be a public HTTPS URL (SSRF blocklist rejects private/loopback/link-local).

input_files.description: Encrypted file-job declarations. Each file is at most 50 MiB and the aggregate is at most 100 MiB.

size_bytes.description: Plaintext size; encrypted wire size is this value plus 60 bytes.

InputUpload.description: Present on authenticated status responses once the bound worker key has verified attestation evidence.

status_url.description: Relative URL to poll for status. Absent on idempotent replay.

job_access_token.description: Returned only once on initial acceptance; never returned on idempotent replay.

idempotent_replay.description: True when this response reflects an existing job whose `client_req_id` was already submitted.

error.description: Failure reason when state is `failed` or `timeout`.

webhook_status.description: HTTP status of the last webhook delivery (or `error: <msg>`).

stripe_pi_amount_cents.description: Original held amount in cents (from the Stripe PaymentIntent).

JobResult.description: Result envelope returned by the worker. All hashes are 64-char hex SHA-256. The result is signed twice — once by the worker (Ed25519) and once by the broker (Ed25519) — over the canonical `{result_hash, skill_hash, input_hash}` triple.

skill_hash.description: SHA-256 of the skill (registered skill's `wasm_manifest_hash`, or `sha256(skill_name)` for built-in stubs).

output.description: Plaintext LLM output. Redacted to `[encrypted — see result_encrypted]` whenever the worker successfully encrypted the result to `result_pubkey`. Set `BROKER_KEEP_PLAINTEXT_FOR_DEMO=1` to keep the plaintext (demo mode only).

fuel_used.description: Mock fuel counter (1 unit / ms). Replace with WASM fuel metering when WASM execution is wired up.

result_encrypted.description: Base64 `ephemeral_pubkey_32 || nonce_12 || ciphertext`. Decrypt with ChaCha20-Poly1305 using `X25519(requester_priv, ephemeral_pubkey)` and AAD `verdantforged-result`.

result_pubkey_ephemeral.description: Base64 32-byte worker ephemeral X25519 pubkey (forward-secrecy: ephemeral keypair is discarded after this job).

worker_signature.description: Base64 Ed25519 signature over `result_hash | skill_hash | input_hash` using the worker's signing key.

broker_signature.description: Base64 Ed25519 signature over the same payload using the broker's signing key. Authoritative non-repudiation root.

billing.description: Echo of `_billing` from the LLM proxy.

llm_error.description: When the LLM proxy returned 5xx, this is the upstream error string.

Payment.description: Stripe PaymentIntent outcome. Surfaced on every finalized job so clients can confirm capture/refund without a second Stripe call.

held_amount_cents.description: Original held amount on the PaymentIntent.

ttl_hours.description: S3 object lifetime (24h via lifecycle rule).

primary.description: The primary LLM output, always attached as the first file.

manifest_url.description: Relative URL to fetch this manifest (same shape, with fresh presigned URLs).

download_urls.description: Map of filename → relative URL. Convenience over iterating `files`.

note.description: Client-facing note explaining how to decrypt and when the URLs expire.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the plaintext file (before encryption).

download_url.description: Presigned S3 GET URL (15-min TTL). Only present on `/v1/jobs/{id}/artifacts` responses.

name.description: Lowercase slug, 3-64 chars, no leading/trailing dash.

ChatCompletionRequest.description: OpenAI-compatible request. The broker whitelists exactly `model`, `messages`, `max_tokens` (capped at 100000), and `stream` (forced to false). All other fields are stripped.

model.description: Ignored — the broker always uses its configured model.

ChatCompletionResponse.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completion response, plus a `_billing` metadata block appended by the broker.

llm_tokens_used.description: Cumulative total tokens used across all worker LLM proxy calls during this job.
